When Anti-Vaccine Activism Takes on the Logic of a Death Cult
By Wayne Oudijn

The phrase “death cult” is harsh. Yet, as organized anti-vaccine movements deepen their opposition to evidence, glorify misinformation, and encourage behaviors that predictably lead to preventable deaths, the term feels less metaphorical than diagnostic.

Vaccines are among the most successful public-health interventions in history. The World Health Organization estimates that measles vaccination alone has saved more than 56 million lives since 2000. During the COVID-19 pandemic, The Lancet reported that vaccines prevented roughly 20 million deaths in their first year of rollout. These are not abstractions — they are quantifiable lives preserved through science, logistics, and collective action.

Yet in online spaces where conspiracy replaces consensus, anti-vaccine leaders frame refusal as moral courage and scientific evidence as state propaganda. The result is not principled skepticism but a closed belief system — one that isolates followers from reality, rewards defiance over data, and ultimately sanctifies suffering.

We are already seeing the consequences. Measles outbreaks are returning in schools that had once achieved herd immunity. Whooping cough and polio are reappearing in communities where vaccination rates have fallen. The cost of “personal choice” in this context is borne collectively, especially by infants, the elderly, and the immunocompromised.

It’s important to separate genuine vaccine hesitancy — which can often be eased through trust and clear information — from organized disinformation networks that thrive on outrage and fear. The former needs compassion: the latter demands confrontation.

A society that turns away from life-saving science in favor of tribal identity and grievance politics risks becoming precisely what it claims to resist: a movement that worships freedom while courting death.

Wayne Oudijn is a kidney transplant recipient.

“1. The polio story as you learned it is wrong. It’s one of the most often misunderstood sequence of events in the last two hundred years. I wanted to explain a few things about the disease to help people understand what actually happened.
2. The first modern account of something resembling polio was in 1789. A physician named Michael Underwood described an illness in children he called “Debility of the Lower Extremities.” He attributed it to teething and foul bowels.
3. One of the next mentions was from Louisiana in 1841. A few children came down with paralysis. The supposed cause: teething. Why would teething be associated with paralysis?
4. Various stories appeared throughout the 1800s of children coming down with paralysis, almost always in their legs. Many people called it “teething paralysis,” but others settled on “infantile paralysis.”
5. This was a new phenomenon: Doctors had never seen it before and didn’t know why it was happening. Research began to reveal that the cause of paralysis were lesions on the grey part of the spinal cord.
6. If you developed a lesion on your spinal cord, they called this a “poliomyelitis.” Polio = grey. Myelitis = inflammation of the spinal cord.
7. A poliomyelitis was a lesion on your spinal cord. You could have more than one of them. But they didn’t know why children had begun developing them, seemingly out of nowhere
8. Scientists conducted research on animals by purposefully poisoning them with arsenic, an ingredient of popular medical remedies of the time. The result? Paralysis in their hind legs.

That’s a great and important question — especially for travelers who go abroad often.

Here’s how it works:

✅ If you’ve already completed your primary polio vaccine series (the 3 or 4 childhood doses), you generally only need one lifetime booster dose of the inactivated polio vaccine (IPV) as an adult.

The World Health Organization (WHO) and the U.S. CDC both recommend just one booster in adulthood for travelers to areas where polio still occurs or is a risk.

You don’t need to get a polio shot every year unless a specific country’s entry requirement or your International Certificate of Vaccination (Yellow Card) requires an updated dose for documentation purposes.

✅ For frequent travelers:
If you travel to countries that still have polio outbreaks (for example, parts of Africa or Asia), authorities may ask for a recent vaccination record (within the past 12 months), especially for long stays. In that case, it’s about entry regulation, not your immunity level — your previous booster still protects you, but you might need a new certificate.

Summary:

Immunity-wise: One adult polio booster is enough for life.

Travel-wise: Some countries may require proof of vaccination within 1 year for entry or exit — so check each destination’s rules.

To help identify parasites which are easily found in fecal matter. We have identified the eggs of gastrointestinal nematodes using the microscope.Gastrointestinal nematodes, commonly known as soil-transmitted helminths, are parasites that infect humans through ingestion of contaminated food or water containing parasite eggs. These worms can cause various symptoms, including abdominal pain, diarrhea, weight loss, and anemia. The severity of these symptoms depends on the type of nematode, the duration and intensity of infection, and individual host factors. Some common gastrointestinal nematodes that affect humans include Ascaris lumbricoides, Trichuris trichiura, Necator Americanus, and Ancylostoma duodenale.

Once ingested, the parasite eggs hatch into larvae that migrate through the gut wall and enter the bloodstream. From there, the larvae travel to the lungs via the portal circulation and are coughed up as part of their migration pattern. The larvae are then swallowed again and return to the gut, completing the life cycle.

The effects of gastrointestinal nematodes on human health can vary widely, ranging from mild symptoms like diarrhea and abdominal discomfort to severe complications like eosinophilic meningitis, etc

The history of Polio.
Without question the go-to disease to defend vaccines is polio. Coincidently it's also the greatest lie and medical con job of all time. Toxins Causing "Polio" is well documented. Polio is the virus that never was.

1824: Metal workers had suffered for centuries from a paralysis similar to polio caused by the lead and arsenic in the metals they were working with. English scientist John Cooke observed: 'The fumes from these metals, or the receptance of them in solution into the stomach, often causes paralysis.'

1890: Lead arsenate pesticide started to be sprayed in the US up to 12 times every summer to kill codling moth on apple crops.

1892: Polio outbreaks began to occur in Vermont, an apple growing region. In his report the Government Inspector Dr. Charles Caverly noted that parents reported that some children fell ill after eating fruit. He stated that 'infantile paralysis usually occurred in families with more than one child, and as no efforts were made at isolation it was very certain it was non-contagious' (with only one child in the family having been struck).

1907: Calcium arsenate comes into use primarily on cotton crops.

1908: In a Massachusetts town with three cotton mills and apple orchards, 69 children suddenly fell ill with infantile paralysis.

1909: The UK bans apple imports from the States because of heavy lead arsenate residues.

1921: Franklin D. Roosevelt develops polio after swimming in Bay of Fundy, New Brunswick. Toxicity of water may have been due to pollution run-off.

1943: DDT is introduced, a neurotoxic pesticide. Over the next several years it comes into widespread use in American households. For example, wall paper impregnated with DDT was placed in children's bedrooms.

1943: A polio epidemic in the UK town of Broadstairs, Kent is linked to a local dairy where cows were washed down with DDT.

1944: Albert Sabin reports that a major cause of sickness and death of American troops based in the Philippines was poliomyelitis. US military camps there were sprayed daily with DDT to kill mosquitoes. Neighboring Philippine settlements were not affected.

1944: NIH reports that DDT damages the same anterior horn cells that are damaged in infantile paralysis.

1946: Gebhaedt shows polio seasonality correlates with fruit harvest.

1949: Endocrinologist Dr Morton Biskind, a practitioner and medical researcher, found that DDT causes 'lesions in the spinal cord similar to human polio.'

1950: US Public Health Industrial Hygiene Medical Director, J.G. Townsend, notes the similarity between parathion poisoning and polio and believes that some polio might be caused by eating fruits or vegetables with parathion residues.

1951: Dr. Biskind treats his polio patients as poisoning victims, removing toxins from food and environment, especially DDT contaminated milk and butter. Dr. Biskind writes: 'Although young animals are more susceptible to the effects of DDT than adults, so far as the available literature is concerned, it does not appear that the effects of such concentrations on infants and children have even been considered.'

1949-1951: Other doctors report they are having success treating polio with anti toxins used to treat poisoning, dimercaprol and ascorbic acid. Example: Dr. F. R. Klenner reported: 'In the poliomyelitis epidemic in North Carolina in 1948 60 cases of this disease came under our care... The treatment was massive doses of vitamin C every two to four hours. Children up to four years received vitamin C injection intramuscularly... All patients were clinically well after 72 hours.'

1950: Dr. Biskind presents evidence to the US Congress that pesticides were the major cause of polio epidemics. He is joined by Dr. Ralph Scobey who reported he found clear evidence of poisoning when analyzing chemical traces in the blood of polio victims.

This post is misleading. The 1954 Salk polio vaccine trial (the “Francis Field Trial”) was one of the largest medical studies ever, involving over 1.8 million children. Yes, it showed no significant reduction in non-paralytic polio (the mild, flu-like form), but the real result was clear: the vaccine reduced paralytic polio by roughly 80–90%. That’s why cases of children ending up in iron lungs plummeted after rollout — from over 15,000 paralytic cases a year in the 1950s to near zero within a generation.

Cherry-picking “non-paralytic” outcomes while ignoring the dramatic protection against paralysis is like claiming seatbelts don’t work because they don’t stop fender benders, while leaving out that they save lives in crashes.

If this group is really full of physicians, the fact they don’t grasp such a basic point about trial endpoints and medical informatics shows they’re either willfully misleading people or they’ve forgotten the first lecture of Epidemiology 101.

Consider what would have happened without the vaccine? A resurgence in polio wouldn't just affect unvaccinated individuals. Risk for infection would spike for vulnerable groups, including babies too young to be vaccinated and the estimated 17 million immunocompromised adults who cannot be vaccinated or would remain susceptible despite vaccination.

By the mid-20th century, the poliovirus could be found all over the world and killed or paralysed over half a million people every year. With no cure, and epidemics on the rise, there was an urgent need for a vaccine.

In 1955 the polio vaccine developed by Dr. Salk and colleagues was licensed in the U.S. Before the polio vaccine, the disease had been a major cause of disability in children. About 16,000 cases of polio (paralytic poliomyelitis) occurred each year in the U.S. in the 20th century compared with none in 2023.

The case fatality ratio for paralytic polio is generally  2% to 5% among children and up to 15% to 30% among adolescents and adults.

If vaccinations were stopped, each year about 2.7 million measles deaths worldwide could be expected. 
In the U.S., widespread use of measles vaccine has led to a greater than 99 percent reduction in measles compared with the pre-vaccine era. If we stopped immunization, measles would increase to pre-vaccine levels.       

Further info: Polio vaccine misinformation on social media: turning point in the fight against polio eradication in Pakistan

OXFORD UNI: In 1967, the year before a vaccine was first introduced, there were 460,407 suspected cases of measles in the UK, and 99 people died from the disease.
https://vaccineknowledge.ox.ac.uk/measles

Since the MMR vaccine was introduced in 1988, these conditions have become rare in the UK. However, outbreaks of disease, especially measles, have occurred when the number of people having the vaccine has dropped. The fall in MMR uptake over the last decade explains the rise in measles cases we have subsequently seen in the UK since 2023.

Since the introduction of the measles vaccine in 1968, 20 million measles cases and 4,500 deaths have been prevented in the UK.

Jonas Salk never said his polio vaccine caused polio. He developed an inactivated (killed-virus) polio vaccine and believed it was the safest approach, as a killed virus cannot cause the disease. A major vaccine safety incident, known as the "Cutter incident," happened with his vaccine, but it was due to manufacturing errors, not a flaw in his core design. Salk's killed-virus approach: Salk's vaccine was intentionally created with a killed poliovirus to eliminate the risk of the virus causing paralysis. This contrasted with Albert Sabin's later oral vaccine, which used a live, weakened virus that could, in very rare instances, revert and cause paralysis.
The Cutter incident: In 1955, shortly after the Salk vaccine was approved, a pharmaceutical company named Cutter Laboratories improperly manufactured some batches of the vaccine.
Live virus contamination: The manufacturing process failed to completely kill the poliovirus, which resulted in live, active virus being present in some vaccine doses.
Resulting cases: This led to thousands of children being exposed to the live virus. Over 200 children experienced varying degrees of paralysis, and 10 died. Salk not at fault: Investigations determined that the problem was not Salk's design but a manufacturing defect that did not follow his specific inactivation instructions. Production was quickly suspended, and corrective safety measures were put in place.

Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s claim that the polio vaccine contained a cancer-causing virus is based on the historical contamination of certain vaccines with Simian Virus 40 (SV40). Polio vaccines administered between 1955 and 1963 were inadvertently contaminated with SV40, a virus found in the monkey kidney cells used to produce the vaccines. Once the contamination was discovered, steps were taken to eliminate SV40, and all polio vaccines currently in use are free of the virus. 
While SV40 has been shown to cause tumors in animals, and its DNA has been detected in some human cancers, a definitive causal link between the SV40-contaminated polio vaccines and cancer in humans has not been established. An Institute of Medicine review concluded that the evidence was "inadequate to accept or reject a causal relationship" between the contaminated vaccine and cancer.

⚠️ Clueless 

We don’t live in 1905 bathing in sewage water…”

✅ True: modern sanitation and clean water dramatically reduced many infectious diseases — including polio  before and alongside vaccines.

❌ But incomplete: clean water alone didn’t eliminate polio. Polio spread primarily via the fecal-oral route, and as sanitation improved, paradoxically, average age of infection increased  meaning children were no longer exposed early when maternal antibodies could protect them. This is part of why major outbreaks occurred in wealthier nations in the 1940s–1950s.

📊 “75%+ of ‘polio infections’ have zero symptoms; paralysis <1%.”

✅ Correct: most polio infections are asymptomatic. Only about 0.1–1% result in paralysis.

❌ But significance matters: polio’s paralysis rate may seem small, but in a large population, that still translated to tens of thousands of paralyzed children each year in the pre-vaccine era. In 1952 alone, the U.S. saw >21,000 cases of paralytic polio and 3,000 deaths, even with modern sanitation by that time.

🧬 “Most modern paralytic cases are vaccine-derived.”

✅ True in part: since wild poliovirus has been eliminated in most regions, vaccine-derived poliovirus (VDPV) is now the dominant cause of new cases.

❌ Why: this happens in areas with low vaccination coverage, allowing the weakened live virus from the oral vaccine to circulate and mutate. Countries using the inactivated polio vaccine (IPV)  like the U.S. since 2000 cannot cause VDPV because it uses a killed virus.

☠️ “DDT and pesticides caused the ‘polio spikes.’”.

❌ There’s no credible scientific evidence that DDT or arsenic caused polio.

Polio virus has been isolated from patients and animals long before widespread pesticide use.

Polio epidemics correlated with viral transmission patterns, not pesticide spraying records.

Controlled studies and pathology show viral infection of motor neurons, not chemical toxicity.

💉 “Modern U.S. polio risk = basically zero.”

✅ Yes: thanks to high vaccine coverage, sanitation, and surveillance, the U.S. has been polio-free since 1979.

❌ But that remains true only because of ongoing vaccination. If vaccination rates drop, vaccine-derived strains can reemerge, as seen in New York in 2022, where an unvaccinated adult developed paralytic polio from circulating VDPV.

⚖️ “My job is to make a risk–benefit calculation for my kids.”

✅ Absolutely right: informed consent means understanding both benefits and risks.
But the data show that the benefit of vaccination (preventing lifelong paralysis) far outweighs the tiny risk of vaccine complications, which are millions of times rarer than polio’s natural complications.

Did you know? Filariasis, or elephantiasis, is a mosquito-borne disease caused by filarial worms 🩸.
These worms live in the lymphatic vessels, causing blockage that leads to severe swelling of the legs, arms, or genitals 😔.

🔬 Diagnosis is made by examining blood samples at night to detect microfilariae, or through the more modern Circulating Filarial Antigen (CFA) test.

💊 Treatment combines DEC + Albendazole/Ivermectin, which effectively kills the parasites and prevents further transmission.

✨ Prevention? Easy!
Use mosquito nets while sleeping, protect yourself from mosquito bites, and participate in the annual Mass Drug Administration (MDA) program by your local health center 🙌.

['Danjuma Shehu Ibrahim writes on the urgent need to dispel vaccine misinformation threatening child survival in the region', 'By Danjuma Shehu, Gombe | Oct. 24, 2025', 'A silent killer continues to claim the lives of thousands of children under the age of five in Nigeria’s troubled Northeast. Beyond the well-known challenges of malnutrition, malaria, and insecurity, misinformation and deeply rooted myths about immunisation are contributing directly to preventable child deaths, undermining public health progress and threatening the future of a generation.', 'Despite decades of global advances in immunisation, dangerous misconceptions about vaccines persist in many communities across the Northeast. Some parents still believe that vaccines cause infertility, lead to autism, or are part of a Western plot to harm Muslim populations. Others see vaccination campaigns as satanic or politically motivated.', 'These falsehoods thrive in areas where literacy levels are low, health education is weak, and public trust in government and health authorities has been eroded by years of conflict and neglect. According to UNICEF, only 33 per cent of children in the Northeast received all basic vaccinations by their first birthday in 2023, compared to over 60 per cent in Nigeria’s southern states. This alarming gap leaves millions of children exposed to preventable diseases such as measles, tetanus, and polio.', 'The human cost of vaccine hesitancy is devastating. In 2022, Nigeria accounted for more than half of all global cases of circulating vaccine-derived poliovirus (cVDPV), with most outbreaks traced to under-immunised communities in the Northeast. Periodic measles outbreaks continue to take a heavy toll, with over 5,000 reported cases and more than 200 child deaths in Borno and Yobe states in 2023, according to the World Health Organization (WHO).', 'Healthcare workers in these states speak of tragic scenes — mothers arriving at hospitals with critically ill children who might have been saved through routine immunisation. “We are fighting not just diseases but misinformation and fear,” said Dr. Fatima Bulama, a paediatrician with the Borno State Primary Health Care Board. “Every unvaccinated child is at risk, and every death from a vaccine-preventable disease is a tragedy that could have been avoided.”', 'The region’s health crisis is compounded by poverty, malnutrition, and conflict — a deadly combination that continues to threaten child survival. Adding vaccine-preventable diseases to this mix only deepens the cycle of suffering and loss. Addressing these harmful beliefs is therefore not just a public health priority but a moral obligation.', 'Northeast Nigeria already bears some of the highest child mortality rates on the continent, with the 2023/24 Nigeria Demographic and Health Survey (NDHS) estimating about 160 deaths per 1,000 live births. The cost of inaction is measured not only in lives lost but also in broken families, weakened communities, and overstretched health systems.', 'However, hope is not lost. Experience shows that when communities are engaged meaningfully, change happens. The involvement of trusted religious and traditional leaders has proven particularly effective. In the early 2000s, the active participation of local imams and women’s groups in Kano State helped reverse polio vaccine boycotts, paving the way for Nigeria’s eventual certification as polio-free in 2020.', 'To reverse current trends, the Nigerian government, through the National Primary Health Care Development Agency (NPHCDA), must intensify collaboration with partners such as UNICEF, WHO, Gavi, and civil society organisations. Health workers require improved training, protection, and community support to carry out their lifesaving duties in difficult environments.', 'The media equally has a critical role in shaping public opinion. By countering misinformation with accurate, accessible reporting — especially in local languages — the press can help rebuild trust and promote vaccine confidence. Parents and caregivers, on their part, must embrace the responsibility of safeguarding their children’s health by seeking reliable information and participating actively in immunisation programmes.', 'When children are fully immunised, communities thrive. Outbreaks are prevented, healthcare costs are reduced, and children are free to grow, learn, and reach their full potential. Immunisation is not merely a medical intervention; it is an investment in the social and economic future of the Northeast.', 'Failure to act decisively risks condemning another generation of children to preventable suffering and death. As the Director-General of the World Health Organization, Dr. Tedros Ghebreyesus, aptly stated:', '“Vaccines are one of the most powerful tools in the history of public health. Where vaccines fail to reach children, myths and misinformation often succeed in taking lives.”', 'The time to act is now. Government, religious leaders, health professionals, civil society, and parents must unite to confront the myths and fear that continue to deny children their right to life and health. Protecting the next generation of Northeastern Nigerians requires truth, courage, and collective responsibility. The children deserve nothing less.', 'Danjuma Shehu Ibrahim is the State Coordinator, Association for Orphans and Vulnerable Children in Nigeria (AONN), and a Fellow of the 2025 Niyel Fellowship Cohort.']

#stitch@kumparan  Pada kasus cacingan, infeksi Soil-Transmitted Helminths (STH: Ascaris lumbricoides, Trichuris trichiura, dan hookworm) bisa menyebabkan kematian, meskipun fatalitasnya relatif rendah kalau dibandingkan dengan prevalensinya.

Secara global, STH diperkirakan menyebabkan ribuan kematian per tahun, terutama akibat komplikasi serius seperti obstruksi usus oleh Ascaris dengan tingkat kematian hingga 5% pada kasus yang membutuhkan operasi darurat. 

Dampak klinis terberat termasuk obstruksi usus, anemia berat akibat hookworm—yang pada ibu hamil meningkatkan risiko anemia dengan odds ratio sekitar 2,55—serta sindrom disentri akibat Trichuris. 

Meskipun jarang terjadi  kematian langsung, morbiditas kronis seperti malnutrisi, anemia, dan gangguan tumbuh-kembang menambah beban kesehatan masyarakat (Jourdan et al., 2020).

Sementara itu, Strongyloides stercoralis memiliki risiko kematian jauh lebih tinggi pada individu dengan gangguan imun. Infeksi yang biasanya ringan dapat berubah menjadi hyperinfection syndrome atau disseminated strongyloidiasis, dengan mortalitas dilaporkan mencapai 70–90% jika tidak segera ditangani. 

Penyebaran larva ke paru-paru, otak, dan aliran darah dapat memicu komplikasi berat seperti sepsis, meningitis, hingga syok septik yang pada akhirnya dapat menyebabkan kematian (Buonfrate et al., 2023). 

Jadi, meskipun proporsi kematian akibat STH relatif kecil, infeksi Strongyloides pada kelompok imunosupresi jauh lebih mematikan, sehingga pencegahan, diagnosis dini, dan terapi tepat waktu menjadi kunci untuk menekan risiko fatalitas.

Sumber Ilmiah:

1. Buonfrate, D., Bisanzio, D., Giorli, G., Odermatt, P., Fürst, T., Greenaway, C., French, M., Reithinger, R., Gobbi, F., Montresor, A., Bisoffi, Z., & Olliaro, P. L. (2023). Severe strongyloidiasis: A systematic review and meta-analysis of case reports. Infectious Diseases of Poverty, 12(1), 51. https://doi.org/10.1186/s40249-023-01089-2

2. Jourdan, P. M., Lamberton, P. H. L., Fenwick, A., & Addiss, D. G. (2020). Soil-transmitted helminth infections. The Lancet, 395(10232), 252–265. https://doi.org/10.1016/S0140-6736(19)32331-7

For over ten years, Thorsen was “number one” on the U.S. Health and Human Services (HHS) “most-wanted list.”

He was indicted by a federal grand jury in 2011 on 22 counts of wire fraud and money laundering.

However, he managed to avoid extradition to America after Denmark refused to hand him over.

Now, thanks to an Interpol Red Notice, the fugitive has finally been caught.

The Man Behind Flawed Vaccine Studies

Thorsen’s papers were instrumental in shaping U.S. vaccine policy.

His findings, claiming no link between vaccines and autism, were cited by the Institute of Medicine.

They were notably used to deny settlements to more than 5,000 families in the National Vaccine Injury Compensation Program (VICP).

Critics have long argued the studies were deeply flawed, with some now suggesting outright fabrication.

Dr. James Lyons-Weiler, Ph.D., said in a detailed analysis:

“Results fabrication in the Danish registry results seems all but certain, given the clear evidence of those practices in other studies on the topic of vaccines and autism.”

Thorsen’s research was funded through CDC grants worth over $11 million between 2000 and 2009.

Instead of ensuring rigorous research, prosecutors allege he submitted fraudulent invoices, siphoning hundreds of thousands of dollars into personal accounts.

The stolen money was allegedly spent on purchasing a home in Atlanta, a Harley Davidson motorcycle, cars, and luxury items.

Kennedy: Thorsen Was HHS’s ‘Number One Fugitive’

HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr., who has repeatedly blasted Thorsen’s role in cementing vaccine orthodoxy, confirmed that the fugitive scientist had topped the department’s most-wanted list for years.

“Thorsen has been number one on the Health and Human Services (HHS) most wanted list for the past 10 years,” Kennedy told Breitbart.

The Fallout

The arrest raises serious questions about the entire body of vaccine-autism research tied to Thorsen and his colleagues.

His co-authored Danish registry studies are now under renewed scrutiny for:

Misclassified and shifting diagnostic categories
Reporting biases and conflicts of interest
Distorted data, vulnerable to confounding variables
Critics argue the CDC-backed studies formed the bedrock of the “safe and effective” narrative while potentially hiding evidence of harm.
Lyons-Weiler called for urgent action:

Full transparency of Thorsen’s datasets
Independent replication of all findings
Reexamination of policies built on his work
What Happens Next

Thorsen faces 13 counts of wire fraud and 9 counts of money laundering, each carrying lengthy prison sentences and heavy fines.

U.S. prosecutors are also seeking forfeiture of all property purchased with the stolen money.

If extradited, the trial could expose not only financial fraud, but the possibility of scientific fraud at the heart of vaccine safety claims.

For years, mainstream media dismissed concerns about Thorsen’s work as “conspiracy theories.”

His arrest may now prove critics right and reveal that the “settled science” used to silence vaccine injury claims may itself have been tainted by fraud.

Without question the go-to disease to defend vaccines is polio. Coincidently it's also the greatest lie and medical con job of all time. Toxins Causing "Polio" is well documented. Polio is the virus that never was.

1824: Metal workers had suffered for centuries from a paralysis similar to polio caused by the lead and arsenic in the metals they were working with. English scientist John Cooke observed: 'The fumes from these metals, or the receptance of them in solution into the stomach, often causes paralysis.'

1890: Lead arsenate pesticide started to be sprayed in the US up to 12 times every summer to kill codling moth on apple crops.

1892: Polio outbreaks began to occur in Vermont, an apple growing region. In his report the Government Inspector Dr. Charles Caverly noted that parents reported that some children fell ill after eating fruit. He stated that 'infantile paralysis usually occurred in families with more than one child, and as no efforts were made at isolation it was very certain it was non-contagious' (with only one child in the family having been struck).

1907: Calcium arsenate comes into use primarily on cotton crops.

1908: In a Massachusetts town with three cotton mills and apple orchards, 69 children suddenly fell ill with infantile paralysis.

1909: The UK bans apple imports from the States because of heavy lead arsenate residues.

1921: Franklin D. Roosevelt develops polio after swimming in Bay of Fundy, New Brunswick. Toxicity of water may have been due to pollution run-off.

1943: DDT is introduced, a neurotoxic pesticide. Over the next several years it comes into widespread use in American households. For example, wall paper impregnated with DDT was placed in children's bedrooms.

1943: A polio epidemic in the UK town of Broadstairs, Kent is linked to a local dairy where cows were washed down with DDT.

1944: Albert Sabin reports that a major cause of sickness and death of American troops based in the Philippines was poliomyelitis. US military camps there were sprayed daily with DDT to kill mosquitoes. Neighboring Philippine settlements were not affected.

1944: NIH reports that DDT damages the same anterior horn cells that are damaged in infantile paralysis.

1946: Gebhaedt shows polio seasonality correlates with fruit harvest.

1949: Endocrinologist Dr Morton Biskind, a practitioner and medical researcher, found that DDT causes 'lesions in the spinal cord similar to human polio.'

1950: US Public Health Industrial Hygiene Medical Director, J.G. Townsend, notes the similarity between parathion poisoning and polio and believes that some polio might be caused by eating fruits or vegetables with parathion residues.

1951: Dr. Biskind treats his polio patients as poisoning victims, removing toxins from food and environment, especially DDT contaminated milk and butter. Dr. Biskind writes: 'Although young animals are more susceptible to the effects of DDT than adults, so far as the available literature is concerned, it does not appear that the effects of such concentrations on infants and children have even been considered.'

1949-1951: Other doctors report they are having success treating polio with anti toxins used to treat poisoning, dimercaprol and ascorbic acid. Example: Dr. F. R. Klenner reported: 'In the poliomyelitis epidemic in North Carolina in 1948 60 cases of this disease came under our care... The treatment was massive doses of vitamin C every two to four hours. Children up to four years received vitamin C injection intramuscularly... All patients were clinically well after 72 hours.'

1950: Dr. Biskind presents evidence to the US Congress that pesticides were the major cause of polio epidemics. He is joined by Dr. Ralph Scobey who reported he found clear evidence of poisoning when analyzing chemical traces in the blood of polio victims.

Comment: This was a no no. The viral causation theory was not something to be questioned. The careers of prominent virologists and health authorities were threatened. Biskind and Scobey's ideas were subjected to ridicule.

1953: Clothes are moth-proofed by washing them in EQ-53, a formula containing DDT.

1953: Dr. Biskind writes: 'It was known by 1945 that DDT was stored in the body fat of mammals and appears in their milk... yet far from admitting a causal relationship between DDT and polio that is so obvious, which in any other field of biology would be instantly accepted, virtually the entire apparatus of communication, lay and scientific alike, has been devoted to denying, concealing, suppressing, distorting and attempts to convert into its opposite this overwhelming evidence. Libel, slander, and economic boycott have not been overlooked in this campaign.'

1954: Legislation recognizing the dangers of persistent pesticides is enacted, and a phase out of DDT in the US accelerates along with a shift of sales of DDT to third world countries.

COLOMBO (News 1st); The Director-General of the World Health Organization (WHO), Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, lauded Sri Lanka’s remarkable public health milestones, describing the island nation as a model for progress despite its lower-middle-income status.

“Although Sri Lanka is classified as a lower-middle-income country, it has the health profile of many high-income nations,” Dr. Tedros said, highlighting the country’s near-universal maternal care and immunization coverage.

“Almost every woman who gives birth does so with a skilled birth attendant. Almost every child receives routine immunizations. You have defeated malaria, eliminated lymphatic filariasis, and maternal and neonatal tetanus.”
Sri Lanka’s leadership in tobacco control also earned praise. It was the first country in Asia to ratify the WHO Framework Convention on Tobacco Control and the first to fully implement it.

However, Dr. Tedros acknowledged that WHO itself is navigating turbulent waters. The organization is undergoing a significant downsizing and restructuring process, which he described as “painful,” involving farewells to many long-serving staff members, including those who worked under extreme pressure during the COVID-19 pandemic.

“These colleagues do not deserve to be treated this way,” he said. “Nevertheless, I see this crisis as an opportunity. I’m confident WHO will emerge sharper, more focused on its core mandate, and more independent.”

Dr. Tedros pointed to two major developments at this year’s World Health Assembly as signs of renewed global commitment to WHO: the approval of an increase in assessed contributions to strengthen financial sustainability and the adoption of the WHO Pandemic Agreement.

“The adoption of the WHO Pandemic Agreement was truly historic,” he noted. “It demonstrates that in these divided times, countries can still come together and find shared solutions to shared problems. It shows that multilateralism is alive and well.”

... Targets 1.3 Million in 10 Endemic LGAs

Lagos State Government, through the Ministry of Health and in partnership with a non-governmental Organisation, Evidence Action, on Tuesday, commenced mass deworming for the treatment and prevention of Soil-Transmitted Helminths (STH) in 10 Local Government Areas of the State.

Speaking during the official flag-off of the exercise held at St. Peters Anglican Primary School, Alausa, the Commissioner for Health, Prof. Akin Abayomi explained that Soil-Transmitted Helminths (STH), which is prevalent in school-age children. is a significant infectious public health burden in Nigeria that results from poor sanitation and hygiene conditions.

According to him, STH is a worm infection that interferes with nutrient uptake and can lead to anaemia, malnourishment and impaired mental, physical and cognitive development, which usually pose a serious threat to children’s health, education and productivity.

He said, “Previously, there have been small-scale deworming activities in the State against STH and Schistosomiasis. However, with the support of the Federal Ministry of Health and our supporting partner, Evidence Action, we have set out plans to implement a Statewide deworming treatment for all enrolled and unenrolled school-age children, who are at risk, using a coordinated approach called School-Based Deworming Mass Administration of Medicines”.

The Commissioner, who was represented by the Lagos State Coordinator, Neglected Tropical Diseases, NTD, Mr. Olanrewaju Jenrola, noted that the treatment strategy is based on the prevalence of STH being determined by mapping and scientific survey as spelt out by the guidelines from Nigeria’s Federal Ministry of Health and the World Health Organisation.

Wow, way more fake news than anybody needs!   That’s a great and important question — let’s go step by step so it’s clear how the numbers fit together.

⸻

🧬 1. Polio before the vaccine

Before vaccines, poliomyelitis (polio) was one of the world’s most feared diseases. It mainly affected children and could cause permanent paralysis or death.
 • Peak U.S. cases:
 • In the early 1950s, around 35,000–60,000 Americans a year were paralyzed or killed by polio.
 • The worst year was 1952, with over 57,000 cases, including 3,000 deaths and 21,000 cases of paralysis.
 • Global estimates:
 • In the 1940s–50s, hundreds of thousands of cases occurred worldwide every year — the World Health Organization (WHO) estimates 350,000+ cases annually in 125 countries as recently as 1988.

⸻

💉 2. Polio vaccines developed

Two main vaccines were created:
 • 1955 – Jonas Salk’s inactivated polio vaccine (IPV) — given by injection.
 • 1961 – Albert Sabin’s oral polio vaccine (OPV) — given by mouth, cheaper and easier for mass immunization.

Both worked extremely well.

⸻

🌍 3. In the last 20 years (2005–2025): how many vaccines have been made and used

While the exact production count varies by year and country, the Global Polio Eradication Initiative (GPEI) reports:
 • Over 20 billion doses of oral and inactivated vaccines have been administered worldwide since 2000.
 • More than 2.5 billion children have been vaccinated in over 200 countries during that time.

This has been one of the largest vaccination efforts in human history.

⸻

📉 4. Impact — how many people were saved
 • Polio cases dropped by over 99.9% since 1988.
 • From 350,000 cases in 1988 → to around 30–100 cases per year worldwide today (mostly in Afghanistan and Pakistan).
 • The WHO estimates the vaccines have prevented more than 20 million cases of paralysis and saved about 1.5–2 million lives globally.

⸻

⚰️ 5. Before vaccines: who got sick or died
 • Millions of children were infected annually before vaccines.
 • About 1 in 200 infections led to paralysis.
 • Of those paralyzed, 5–10% died because their breathing muscles were affected.

⸻

Groundbreaking. Researchers at the University of Florida have developed a new mRNA-based cancer vaccine that has shown remarkable results against glioblastoma, one of the most aggressive and fatal brain tumors.

🧠 What Happened

• In a recent clinical trial, four patients with treatment-resistant glioblastoma received the vaccine.

💉 How It Works

• The vaccine uses mRNA technology, similar to COVID-19 vaccines, but instead of targeting a virus, it trains the immune system to recognize and attack cancer cells.
• It stimulates the expression of PD-L1, a protein that makes tumors more visible to immune cells.
• In animal models, the vaccine even wiped out tumors without any other treatment 

🔬 What’s Next

• More people are now being enrolled in expanded trials.
• Researchers hope this could become a universal cancer vaccine, capable of fighting multiple types of tumors.

Mary S. Sherman, a prominent orthopedic surgeon and cancer researcher, was involved in medical work related to polio and its potential long-term health consequences. She served as an associate professor of orthopedic surgery at the University of Chicago, where her research focused on bone cancer and polio treatments.
 In the late 1940s and early 1950s, she traveled to Alaska to conduct medical surveys and provide care in remote communities, including at Mount Edgecumbe Hospital on Kruzof Island.
Her later career included a significant role at the Ochsner Clinic in New Orleans, where she worked on cancer research and was associated with Dr. Alton Ochsner.
 According to conspiracy theories popularized by Edward T. Haslam in his book Dr. Mary's Monkey, Sherman was involved in secret research related to the polio vaccine's contamination with the SV-40 virus, a monkey virus known to cause cancer in humans.
 Haslam theorizes that the contaminated polio vaccine, which was grown on monkey kidney cells, led to an epidemic of soft-tissue cancers, prompting a covert government-backed research program to develop a vaccine to counteract this threat.
 This research allegedly involved the use of a linear particle accelerator at the Public Health Service Hospital in New Orleans.
The theory suggests that Sherman's work on this project, which included experimenting with cancer-causing monkey viruses, led to her murder in July 1964.
 Her body was found severely burned, with her right arm and part of her torso disintegrated, an injury that Haslam claims could not have resulted from a small fire in her apartment.
 He argues that the burn patterns were consistent with an accident involving the linear particle accelerator, and that her death was covered up to conceal the secret research.
 While forensic pathologists dispute this account, stating that a normal fire could have caused the observed burns, the theory remains a central element of the unresolved mystery surrounding her death.
The connection between Sherman and the polio vaccine controversy is further highlighted by the fact that the SV-40 virus, which was present in some early polio vaccines, has been linked to various cancers, including soft-tissue cancers, and has been a subject of ongoing medical debate.
 The controversy surrounding the vaccine's contamination and its potential long-term health effects remains a significant point of discussion in medical history.

['Danjuma Shehu Ibrahim writes on the urgent need to dispel vaccine misinformation threatening child survival in the region', 'By Danjuma Shehu, Gombe | Oct. 24, 2025', 'A silent killer continues to claim the lives of thousands of children under the age of five in Nigeria’s troubled Northeast. Beyond the well-known challenges of malnutrition, malaria, and insecurity, misinformation and deeply rooted myths about immunisation are contributing directly to preventable child deaths, undermining public health progress and threatening the future of a generation.', 'Despite decades of global advances in immunisation, dangerous misconceptions about vaccines persist in many communities across the Northeast. Some parents still believe that vaccines cause infertility, lead to autism, or are part of a Western plot to harm Muslim populations. Others see vaccination campaigns as satanic or politically motivated.', 'These falsehoods thrive in areas where literacy levels are low, health education is weak, and public trust in government and health authorities has been eroded by years of conflict and neglect. According to UNICEF, only 33 per cent of children in the Northeast received all basic vaccinations by their first birthday in 2023, compared to over 60 per cent in Nigeria’s southern states. This alarming gap leaves millions of children exposed to preventable diseases such as measles, tetanus, and polio.', 'The human cost of vaccine hesitancy is devastating. In 2022, Nigeria accounted for more than half of all global cases of circulating vaccine-derived poliovirus (cVDPV), with most outbreaks traced to under-immunised communities in the Northeast. Periodic measles outbreaks continue to take a heavy toll, with over 5,000 reported cases and more than 200 child deaths in Borno and Yobe states in 2023, according to the World Health Organization (WHO).', 'Healthcare workers in these states speak of tragic scenes — mothers arriving at hospitals with critically ill children who might have been saved through routine immunisation. “We are fighting not just diseases but misinformation and fear,” said Dr. Fatima Bulama, a paediatrician with the Borno State Primary Health Care Board. “Every unvaccinated child is at risk, and every death from a vaccine-preventable disease is a tragedy that could have been avoided.”', 'The region’s health crisis is compounded by poverty, malnutrition, and conflict — a deadly combination that continues to threaten child survival. Adding vaccine-preventable diseases to this mix only deepens the cycle of suffering and loss. Addressing these harmful beliefs is therefore not just a public health priority but a moral obligation.', 'Northeast Nigeria already bears some of the highest child mortality rates on the continent, with the 2023/24 Nigeria Demographic and Health Survey (NDHS) estimating about 160 deaths per 1,000 live births. The cost of inaction is measured not only in lives lost but also in broken families, weakened communities, and overstretched health systems.', 'However, hope is not lost. Experience shows that when communities are engaged meaningfully, change happens. The involvement of trusted religious and traditional leaders has proven particularly effective. In the early 2000s, the active participation of local imams and women’s groups in Kano State helped reverse polio vaccine boycotts, paving the way for Nigeria’s eventual certification as polio-free in 2020.', 'To reverse current trends, the Nigerian government, through the National Primary Health Care Development Agency (NPHCDA), must intensify collaboration with partners such as UNICEF, WHO, Gavi, and civil society organisations. Health workers require improved training, protection, and community support to carry out their lifesaving duties in difficult environments.', 'The media equally has a critical role in shaping public opinion. By countering misinformation with accurate, accessible reporting — especially in local languages — the press can help rebuild trust and promote vaccine confidence. Parents and caregivers, on their part, must embrace the responsibility of safeguarding their children’s health by seeking reliable information and participating actively in immunisation programmes.', 'When children are fully immunised, communities thrive. Outbreaks are prevented, healthcare costs are reduced, and children are free to grow, learn, and reach their full potential. Immunisation is not merely a medical intervention; it is an investment in the social and economic future of the Northeast.', 'Failure to act decisively risks condemning another generation of children to preventable suffering and death. As the Director-General of the World Health Organization, Dr. Tedros Ghebreyesus, aptly stated:', '“Vaccines are one of the most powerful tools in the history of public health. Where vaccines fail to reach children, myths and misinformation often succeed in taking lives.”', 'The time to act is now. Government, religious leaders, health professionals, civil society, and parents must unite to confront the myths and fear that continue to deny children their right to life and health. Protecting the next generation of Northeastern Nigerians requires truth, courage, and collective responsibility. The children deserve nothing less.', 'Danjuma Shehu Ibrahim is the State Coordinator, Association for Orphans and Vulnerable Children in Nigeria (AONN), and a Fellow of the 2025 Niyel Fellowship Cohort.']

Consider what would have happened without the vaccine? A resurgence in polio wouldn't just affect unvaccinated individuals. Risk for infection would spike for vulnerable groups, including babies too young to be vaccinated and the estimated 17 million immunocompromised adults who cannot be vaccinated or would remain susceptible despite vaccination.

By the mid-20th century, the poliovirus could be found all over the world and killed or paralysed over half a million people every year. With no cure, and epidemics on the rise, there was an urgent need for a vaccine.

In 1955 the polio vaccine developed by Dr. Salk and colleagues was licensed in the U.S. Before the polio vaccine, the disease had been a major cause of disability in children. About 16,000 cases of polio (paralytic poliomyelitis) occurred each year in the U.S. in the 20th century compared with none in 2023.

The case fatality ratio for paralytic polio is generally  2% to 5% among children and up to 15% to 30% among adolescents and adults.

If vaccinations were stopped, each year about 2.7 million measles deaths worldwide could be expected. 
In the U.S., widespread use of measles vaccine has led to a greater than 99 percent reduction in measles compared with the pre-vaccine era. If we stopped immunization, measles would increase to pre-vaccine levels.

That claim is misleading and taken out of context. Here’s what really happened:

In 1954, Jonas Salk’s inactivated polio vaccine (IPV) underwent the largest medical field trial ever conducted at that time, organized by the National Foundation for Infantile Paralysis (March of Dimes).
About 1.3 million children (called “Polio Pioneers”) were enrolled. Roughly 400,000 received the vaccine, 200,000 received a placebo, and the rest were observed without intervention.
The results, published in April 1955, showed:
Polio cases (paralytic) were much lower among vaccinated children than placebo groups.
Vaccine efficacy was about 80–90% against paralytic polio.
Some internal trial reports did note no significant reduction in non-paralytic polio. The critical outcome was paralysis, and in that respect the vaccine was dramatically effective.

Bottom line:
Yes, it’s true that the 1954 trial found no significant difference in non-paralytic polio rates. But that’s misleading if presented without context — the vaccine’s success was measured by preventing paralysis, and on that front it was a major victory.

To help identify parasites which are easily found in fecal matter. We have identified the eggs of gastrointestinal nematodes using the microscope.Gastrointestinal nematodes, commonly known as soil-transmitted helminths, are parasites that infect humans through ingestion of contaminated food or water containing parasite eggs. These worms can cause various symptoms, including abdominal pain, diarrhea, weight loss, and anemia. The severity of these symptoms depends on the type of nematode, the duration and intensity of infection, and individual host factors. Some common gastrointestinal nematodes that affect humans include Ascaris lumbricoides, Trichuris trichiura, Necator Americanus, and Ancylostoma duodenale.

Once ingested, the parasite eggs hatch into larvae that migrate through the gut wall and enter the bloodstream. From there, the larvae travel to the lungs via the portal circulation and are coughed up as part of their migration pattern. The larvae are then swallowed again and return to the gut, completing the life cycle.

The effects of gastrointestinal nematodes on human health can vary widely, ranging from mild symptoms like diarrhea and abdominal discomfort to severe complications like eosinophilic meningitis, etc

The history of Polio.
Without question the go-to disease to defend vaccines is polio. Coincidently it's also the greatest lie and medical con job of all time. Toxins Causing "Polio" is well documented. Polio is the virus that never was.

1824: Metal workers had suffered for centuries from a paralysis similar to polio caused by the lead and arsenic in the metals they were working with. English scientist John Cooke observed: 'The fumes from these metals, or the receptance of them in solution into the stomach, often causes paralysis.'

1890: Lead arsenate pesticide started to be sprayed in the US up to 12 times every summer to kill codling moth on apple crops.

1892: Polio outbreaks began to occur in Vermont, an apple growing region. In his report the Government Inspector Dr. Charles Caverly noted that parents reported that some children fell ill after eating fruit. He stated that 'infantile paralysis usually occurred in families with more than one child, and as no efforts were made at isolation it was very certain it was non-contagious' (with only one child in the family having been struck).

1907: Calcium arsenate comes into use primarily on cotton crops.

1908: In a Massachusetts town with three cotton mills and apple orchards, 69 children suddenly fell ill with infantile paralysis.

1909: The UK bans apple imports from the States because of heavy lead arsenate residues.

1921: Franklin D. Roosevelt develops polio after swimming in Bay of Fundy, New Brunswick. Toxicity of water may have been due to pollution run-off.

1943: DDT is introduced, a neurotoxic pesticide. Over the next several years it comes into widespread use in American households. For example, wall paper impregnated with DDT was placed in children's bedrooms.

1943: A polio epidemic in the UK town of Broadstairs, Kent is linked to a local dairy where cows were washed down with DDT.

1944: Albert Sabin reports that a major cause of sickness and death of American troops based in the Philippines was poliomyelitis. US military camps there were sprayed daily with DDT to kill mosquitoes. Neighboring Philippine settlements were not affected.

1944: NIH reports that DDT damages the same anterior horn cells that are damaged in infantile paralysis.

1946: Gebhaedt shows polio seasonality correlates with fruit harvest.

1949: Endocrinologist Dr Morton Biskind, a practitioner and medical researcher, found that DDT causes 'lesions in the spinal cord similar to human polio.'

1950: US Public Health Industrial Hygiene Medical Director, J.G. Townsend, notes the similarity between parathion poisoning and polio and believes that some polio might be caused by eating fruits or vegetables with parathion residues.

1951: Dr. Biskind treats his polio patients as poisoning victims, removing toxins from food and environment, especially DDT contaminated milk and butter. Dr. Biskind writes: 'Although young animals are more susceptible to the effects of DDT than adults, so far as the available literature is concerned, it does not appear that the effects of such concentrations on infants and children have even been considered.'

1949-1951: Other doctors report they are having success treating polio with anti toxins used to treat poisoning, dimercaprol and ascorbic acid. Example: Dr. F. R. Klenner reported: 'In the poliomyelitis epidemic in North Carolina in 1948 60 cases of this disease came under our care... The treatment was massive doses of vitamin C every two to four hours. Children up to four years received vitamin C injection intramuscularly... All patients were clinically well after 72 hours.'

1950: Dr. Biskind presents evidence to the US Congress that pesticides were the major cause of polio epidemics. He is joined by Dr. Ralph Scobey who reported he found clear evidence of poisoning when analyzing chemical traces in the blood of polio victims.

Comment: This was a no no. The viral causation theory was not something to be questioned. The careers of prominent virologists and health authorities were threatened. Biskind and Scobey's ideas were subjected to ridicule.

1953: Clothes are moth-proofed by washing them in EQ-53, a formula containing DDT.

1953: Dr. Biskind writes: 'It was known by 1945 that DDT was stored in the body fat of mammals and appears in their milk... yet far from admitting a causal relationship between DDT and polio that is so obvious, which in any other field of biology would be instantly accepted, virtually the entire apparatus of communication, lay and scientific alike, has been devoted to denying, concealing, suppressing, distorting and attempts to convert into its opposite this overwhelming evidence. Libel, slander, and economic boycott have not been overlooked in this campaign.'

1954: Legislation recognizing the dangers of persistent pesticides is enacted, and a phase out of DDT in the US accelerates along with a shift of sales of DDT to third world countries.

(Note that DDT is phased out at the same time as widespread polio vaccinations begin. Saying that, polio cases sky rocket only in communities that accept the polio vaccine, as the polio vaccine is laced with heavy metals and other toxins, so the paralysis narrative starts all over again.  As the polio vaccines cause huge spikes in polio, the misinformed public demand more polio vaccine and the cycle spirals skyward exponentially)

1956: the American Medical Association mandated that all licensed medical doctors could no longer classify polio as polio. All polio diagnosis would be rejected in favor of Guillian-Barre Syndrome, AFP (acute flaccid paralysis), Bell's Palsy, Cerebral Palsy, ALS, (Lou-Gehrig's Disease), MS, MD etc etc. This sleight of hand was fabricated with the sole intent of giving the public the impression that the polio vaccine was successful at decreasing polio or eradicating polio. The public bought this hook, line and sinker and to this very day, many pro vaccine arguments are ignited by the manufactured lie regarding the polio vaccine eradicating polio.

1962: Rachel Carson's Silent Spring is published.

1968: DDT registration cancelled for the US.

2008: Acute Flaccid Paralysis (AFP) is still a raging in many parts of the world where pesticide use is high, and DDT is still used. AFP. MS, MD, Bell's Palsy, cerebral palsy, ALS (Lou Gehrig's Disease), Guillian-Barre are all catch basket diagnosis, all similar in symtpoms, tied to heavy metal poisoning and high toxic load. 

2008: WHO states on its website: 'There is no cure for polio. Its effects are irreversible.'

Public funds are wasted on useless and dangerous vaccines when the children could be treated with antitoxins. “Notorious Vaccine Failures – Historical Examples
There is no single vaccine universally recognized as the 'worst of all time,' because different vaccines have failed for different reasons—sometimes due to scientific limitations of the era, sometimes due to poor trial design, manufacturing errors, or political misuse. However, a few vaccines stand out historically for their notable failures in effectiveness, safety, or both.

1. 1960s RSV (Respiratory Syncytial Virus) Vaccine
Why it failed: In 1966, a formalin-inactivated RSV vaccine was given to children. Not only did it fail to protect them, it caused enhanced disease when they later encountered RSV naturally.

Impact: 80% of vaccinated children in trials were hospitalized when they caught RSV, and two died.

Lesson: A vaccine can make a disease worse if the immune response it triggers is not the right kind.

2. 1976 “Swine Flu” Vaccine (U.S.)
Why it failed: The U.S. government rushed to vaccinate ~45 million people against a swine flu strain feared to cause a pandemic. That pandemic never materialized.

Impact: The vaccine was linked to an increased risk of Guillain-Barré syndrome (a rare paralytic condition), leading to public mistrust.

Lesson: Premature mass vaccination without strong evidence of necessity can cause more harm than benefit.

3. Dengvaxia (Dengue Fever Vaccine) – Philippines
Why it failed: Approved in 2015, it was given to ~800,000 schoolchildren. Later data showed it increased the risk of severe dengue in those who had never been previously infected.

Impact: Dozens of deaths were politically attributed to the vaccine, leading to criminal charges and a collapse of public trust in vaccination programs nationwide.

Lesson: Pre-screening for prior infection is crucial when vaccine risks depend on immune history.

4. Cutter Incident – 1955 Polio Vaccine (Salk)
Why it failed: A manufacturing error at Cutter Laboratories led to polio vaccines that contained live poliovirus.

Impact: 40,000 cases of polio, 200 cases of paralysis, and 10 deaths.

Lesson: Even an effective vaccine can be a disaster if quality control fails.

Examples of Low-Effectiveness Vaccines
• Bacillus Calmette–Guérin (BCG) Tuberculosis Vaccine — offers inconsistent protection in adults (0–80% efficacy depending on region) but still widely given in many countries.

• Current influenza vaccines — effectiveness often hovers between 10–60% depending on the year’s strain match.”

Paralytic polio rates are extremely low in vaccinated populations, though very rare cases of vaccine-associated paralytic poliomyelitis (VAPP) can occur with the oral polio vaccine (OPV). These VAPP cases happen in approximately 1 in 2.7 million OPV doses, more often with the first dose. The risk is much lower with inactivated polio vaccine (IPV), which is a "killed" vaccine and does not use live virus. 
Understanding the Rare Cases of Paralytic Polio After Vaccination
Oral Polio Vaccine (OPV):
This vaccine contains a weakened live virus that, in very rare instances, can revert to a paralytic form, leading to VAPP. This is the most common source of paralytic polio cases in highly vaccinated populations. 
Vaccine-Derived Poliovirus (VDPV):
When the OPV virus reverts to a form that can spread and cause paralysis, it's called vaccine-derived poliovirus (VDPV). These outbreaks are linked to low vaccination rates, allowing the virus to spread. 
The Role of Vaccination in Reducing Paralytic Polio
Near Elimination of Wild Polio:
The global introduction of polio vaccines has been highly successful in nearly eliminating wild poliovirus, especially after WPV2 and WPV3 were eradicated. 
High Vaccine Effectiveness:
With high vaccination coverage, the risk of wild or vaccine-derived paralytic polio becomes extremely low. 
Shift to Inactivated Polio Vaccine (IPV):
Public health strategies are shifting to IPV in many areas because it uses a "killed" virus and cannot cause VAPP. 
Why Paralytic Polio Can Still Occur
VAPP and Low Coverage:
The risk of VAPP exists with the oral vaccine. When immunization rates fall, even the rare VAPP-causing virus can spread and cause outbreaks. 
Provocation Paralysis:
A rare phenomenon, provocation paralysis, can occur after intramuscular injections, such as shots given for other illnesses, shortly after a child receives the oral polio vaccine, according to a study from PubMed. 
In essence, paralysis after vaccination is exceptionally rare and primarily linked to the live-attenuated OPV. However, this risk is significantly outweighed by the benefit of preventing polio through widespread vaccination.

#stitch@kumparan  Pada kasus cacingan, infeksi Soil-Transmitted Helminths (STH: Ascaris lumbricoides, Trichuris trichiura, dan hookworm) bisa menyebabkan kematian, meskipun fatalitasnya relatif rendah kalau dibandingkan dengan prevalensinya.

Secara global, STH diperkirakan menyebabkan ribuan kematian per tahun, terutama akibat komplikasi serius seperti obstruksi usus oleh Ascaris dengan tingkat kematian hingga 5% pada kasus yang membutuhkan operasi darurat. 

Dampak klinis terberat termasuk obstruksi usus, anemia berat akibat hookworm—yang pada ibu hamil meningkatkan risiko anemia dengan odds ratio sekitar 2,55—serta sindrom disentri akibat Trichuris. 

Meskipun jarang terjadi  kematian langsung, morbiditas kronis seperti malnutrisi, anemia, dan gangguan tumbuh-kembang menambah beban kesehatan masyarakat (Jourdan et al., 2020).

Sementara itu, Strongyloides stercoralis memiliki risiko kematian jauh lebih tinggi pada individu dengan gangguan imun. Infeksi yang biasanya ringan dapat berubah menjadi hyperinfection syndrome atau disseminated strongyloidiasis, dengan mortalitas dilaporkan mencapai 70–90% jika tidak segera ditangani. 

Penyebaran larva ke paru-paru, otak, dan aliran darah dapat memicu komplikasi berat seperti sepsis, meningitis, hingga syok septik yang pada akhirnya dapat menyebabkan kematian (Buonfrate et al., 2023). 

Jadi, meskipun proporsi kematian akibat STH relatif kecil, infeksi Strongyloides pada kelompok imunosupresi jauh lebih mematikan, sehingga pencegahan, diagnosis dini, dan terapi tepat waktu menjadi kunci untuk menekan risiko fatalitas.

Great question — and yes, there’s a difference between the polio vaccines commonly used in Africa and those used in Europe or the West, though both protect against the same virus.

Here’s the breakdown 👇

---

🌍 In Africa (and many developing countries):

Most countries use the Oral Polio Vaccine (OPV) — the drops given by mouth.

It contains weakened live poliovirus.

It’s cheap, easy to give (no injection), and provides gut immunity, which helps stop the spread of the virus in communities.

However, very rarely, the live virus can mutate and cause “vaccine-derived polio” in under-immunized populations — which is why some countries are slowly switching away from it.

In some African countries (like Kenya, Nigeria, Ghana, etc.), babies now also receive both OPV and IPV (injection) as part of the new “polio eradication strategy.”

Typical schedule in Africa:

At birth: OPV “zero dose”

6 weeks: OPV + IPV

10 weeks: OPV

14 weeks: OPV

Sometimes a booster at 9 months or later.

---

🌍 In Europe, the US, and other Western countries:

They use only the Inactivated Polio Vaccine (IPV) — the injection form.

It contains killed (inactivated) virus — so it can’t mutate or cause polio.

📌How is Onchocerciasis Diagnosed?

- Skin Snip Biopsy: A small piece of skin is examined under a microscope to detect microfilariae.

- Nodule Examination: Doctors may identify worm-filled nodules under the skin.

- Eye Exams: To check for eye damage or microfilariae in the eyes.

- Blood Tests: Antibody tests can detect past or current infections.

- Slit-Lamp Exam: Used to look for eye involvement, especially in advanced cases.

Early diagnosis is key to preventing severe complications!

That claim is misleading and taken out of context. Here’s what really happened:

In 1954, Jonas Salk’s inactivated polio vaccine (IPV) underwent the largest medical field trial ever conducted at that time, organized by the National Foundation for Infantile Paralysis (March of Dimes).
About 1.3 million children (called “Polio Pioneers”) were enrolled. Roughly 400,000 received the vaccine, 200,000 received a placebo, and the rest were observed without intervention.
The results, published in April 1955, showed:
Polio cases (paralytic) were much lower among vaccinated children than placebo groups.
Vaccine efficacy was about 80–90% against paralytic polio.
Some internal trial reports did note no significant reduction in non-paralytic polio. The critical outcome was paralysis, and in that respect the vaccine was dramatically effective.

Bottom line:
Yes, it’s true that the 1954 trial found no significant difference in non-paralytic polio rates. But that’s misleading if presented without context — the vaccine’s success was measured by preventing paralysis, and on that front it was a major victory.

For those too easily manipulated to actually fact check him, here you go...

Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s claim that the polio vaccine contained a cancer-causing virus is based on the historical contamination of certain vaccines with Simian Virus 40 (SV40). Polio vaccines administered between 1955 and 1963 were inadvertently contaminated with SV40, a virus found in the monkey kidney cells used to produce the vaccines. Once the contamination was discovered, steps were taken to eliminate SV40, and all polio vaccines currently in use are free of the virus. 
While SV40 has been shown to cause tumors in animals, and its DNA has been detected in some human cancers, a definitive causal link between the SV40-contaminated polio vaccines and cancer in humans has not been established. An Institute of Medicine review concluded that the evidence was "inadequate to accept or reject a causal relationship" between the contaminated vaccine and cancer.

Polio’s resurgence: A self-inflicted epidemic

The polio vaccine was marketed as a miracle cure, but behind the scenes, it was a public health disaster.

Polio cases surged after vaccination campaigns. Massachusetts saw a 642% increase in 1955, while Vermont experienced a 266% spike—all linked to the vaccine.

By the 1970s, Dr. Jonas Salk himself admitted that the oral polio vaccine was causing polio. CDC data later confirmed that 100% of U.S. polio cases by 1989 were vaccine-induced.

Health officials changed diagnostic criteria to hide the truth. Before 1954, polio was diagnosed after 24 hours of paralysis; post-vaccine, the requirement was extended to 60 days, artificially reducing case numbers.

The oral vaccine’s live virus spread through fecal matter, infecting unvaccinated children and causing outbreaks in communities.

VAERS reports reveal 13,641 adverse events—including 540 deaths—linked to the polio vaccine in just five years.

The vaccine didn’t eradicate polio—it redefined it, replacing wild polio with vaccine-derived strains. Today, as global health agencies push new polio boosters, the question remains: Will history repeat itself?

Great question — and yes, there’s a difference between the polio vaccines commonly used in Africa and those used in Europe or the West, though both protect against the same virus.

Here’s the breakdown 👇

---

🌍 In Africa (and many developing countries):

Most countries use the Oral Polio Vaccine (OPV) — the drops given by mouth.

It contains weakened live poliovirus.

It’s cheap, easy to give (no injection), and provides gut immunity, which helps stop the spread of the virus in communities.

However, very rarely, the live virus can mutate and cause “vaccine-derived polio” in under-immunized populations — which is why some countries are slowly switching away from it.

In some African countries (like Kenya, Nigeria, Ghana, etc.), babies now also receive both OPV and IPV (injection) as part of the new “polio eradication strategy.”

Typical schedule in Africa:

At birth: OPV “zero dose”

6 weeks: OPV + IPV

10 weeks: OPV

14 weeks: OPV

Sometimes a booster at 9 months or later.

---

🌍 In Europe, the US, and other Western countries:

They use only the Inactivated Polio Vaccine (IPV) — the injection form.

It contains killed (inactivated) virus — so it can’t mutate or cause polio.
